audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Leviticus / Vayiqra scale-affliction cleansing and house-affliction text tradition, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for cleansing of scale-afflicted person, birds/wood/scarlet/hyssop rite, shaving/washing, offerings with poverty option, house affliction inspection/remediation/cleansing.

verses.restored: YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,
This shall be Torah/instruction of one afflicted with scale-disease/tzaraat in day of his cleansing: he shall be brought to priest.
Priest shall go out to outside camp, and priest shall look; if affliction of scale-disease is healed from afflicted one,
priest shall command, and for the one being cleansed they shall take two living clean birds, cedar wood, scarlet, and hyssop.
Priest shall command, and one bird shall be slaughtered into earthen vessel over living water.
The living bird he shall take, and cedar wood, scarlet, and hyssop, and dip them and living bird in blood/life of bird slaughtered over living water.
He shall sprinkle on one being cleansed from scale-disease seven times, declare him clean, and send living bird over face of field.
The one being cleansed shall wash garments, shave off all hair, wash in water, and be clean; afterward he may come into camp but dwell outside his tent seven days.
On seventh day he shall shave all his hair, head, beard, eyebrows, all hair he shall shave; wash garments, wash flesh in water, and be clean.
On eighth day he shall take two complete male lambs, one complete female lamb daughter of a year, three-tenths fine flour mixed with oil for grain offering, and one log of oil.
Priest who cleanses shall stand the man being cleansed and these things before YHWH at entrance of tent of meeting.
Priest shall take one male lamb and bring it near for guilt/reparation offering with log of oil, and wave them as wave offering before YHWH.

He shall slaughter lamb in place where sin/misalignment offering and burnt offering are slaughtered, in holy place, for guilt/reparation offering like sin/misalignment offering belongs to priest; Holy of Holies it is.
Priest shall take from blood/life of guilt/reparation offering and put on lobe of right ear of one being cleansed, on thumb of right hand, and on big toe of right foot.
Priest shall take from log of oil and pour into left palm of priest.
Priest shall dip his right finger in oil that is in his left palm and sprinkle from oil with his finger seven times before YHWH.
From remainder of oil in his palm priest shall put on lobe of right ear of one being cleansed, on thumb of right hand, and on big toe of right foot, upon blood/life of guilt/reparation offering.
Remaining oil in priest's palm he shall put on head of one being cleansed; priest shall make atonement/covering for him before YHWH.
Priest shall make sin/misalignment offering and make atonement/covering for one being cleansed from his uncleanness; afterward he shall slaughter burnt offering.
Priest shall bring up burnt offering and grain offering on altar; priest shall make atonement/covering for him, and he shall be clean.

If he is poor and his hand does not reach, he shall take one male lamb for guilt/reparation offering to wave, to make atonement/covering for him, one-tenth fine flour mixed with oil for grain offering, a log of oil,
and two turtledoves or two sons of pigeon, as his hand reaches; one shall be sin/misalignment offering and one burnt offering.
On eighth day he shall bring them for his cleansing to priest, to entrance of tent of meeting, before YHWH.
Priest shall take lamb of guilt/reparation offering and log of oil, and priest shall wave them as wave offering before YHWH.

He shall slaughter lamb of guilt/reparation offering, and priest shall take from blood/life of guilt/reparation offering and put on lobe of right ear of one being cleansed, on thumb of right hand, and big toe of right foot.
Priest shall pour from oil into left palm of priest.
Priest shall sprinkle with right finger from oil in his left palm seven times before YHWH.
Priest shall put from oil in his palm on lobe of right ear of one being cleansed, on thumb of right hand, and on big toe of right foot, on place of blood/life of guilt/reparation offering.
What remains from oil in priest's palm he shall put on head of one being cleansed, to make atonement/covering for him before YHWH.
He shall offer one of turtledoves or sons of pigeon, from what his hand reaches,
what his hand reaches, one for sin/misalignment offering and one for burnt offering with grain offering; priest shall make atonement/covering for one being cleansed before YHWH.
This is Torah/instruction for one in whom affliction of scale-disease is, whose hand does not reach for his cleansing.
YHWH spoke to Moses and Aaron, saying,
When you come into land of Canaan, which I give to you as possession, and I put affliction of scale-disease in house of land of your possession,
the one whose house it is shall come and tell priest, saying, Something like affliction has appeared to me in house.
Priest shall command that they empty house before priest comes to look at affliction, so all that is in house not become unclean; afterward priest shall come to look at house.
He shall look at affliction, and if affliction in walls of house has greenish or reddish depressions, and their appearance is deeper than wall,
priest shall go out from house to entrance of house and shut up house seven days.
Priest shall return on seventh day and look; if affliction has spread in walls of house,
priest shall command, and they shall pull out stones in which affliction is and throw them outside city to unclean place.
He shall have house scraped inside all around, and they shall pour dust that they scraped outside city to unclean place.
They shall take other stones and bring them instead of those stones; other plaster he shall take and plaster house.
If affliction returns and breaks out in house after stones were pulled out, after house was scraped, and after plastered,
priest shall come and look; if affliction has spread in house, malignant scale-disease is in house; it is unclean.
He shall tear down house, its stones, wood, and all plaster of house, and bring them outside city to unclean place.
One entering house all days it is shut up shall be unclean until evening.
One lying in house shall wash garments; one eating in house shall wash garments.
But if priest comes and looks, and look, affliction has not spread in house after house was plastered, priest shall declare house clean, for affliction is healed.
To cleanse house he shall take two birds, cedar wood, scarlet, and hyssop.
He shall slaughter one bird into earthen vessel over living water.
He shall take cedar wood, hyssop, scarlet, and living bird, dip them in blood/life of slaughtered bird and in living water, and sprinkle house seven times.
He shall cleanse house with blood/life of bird, living water, living bird, cedar wood, hyssop, and scarlet.
He shall send living bird outside city over face of field. He shall make atonement/covering for house, and it shall be clean.
This is Torah/instruction for every affliction of scale-disease and for scall,
and for scale-disease of garment and of house,
and for swelling, scab, and bright spot,
to teach in day of uncleanness and day of cleanness. This is Torah/instruction of scale-disease.

translation_notes: Priest goes outside camp to inspect healing, showing reintegration begins by meeting the separated person where they are.
Bird/cedar/scarlet/hyssop/living-water rite is complex symbolic cleansing; avoid over-allegorizing in source layer.
Ear/thumb/toe oil and blood echo priestly ordination, suggesting restoration to embodied life and community.
House affliction demonstrates tzaraat is not simply human disease; it includes material/environmental affliction.
safety_note: Do not use this chapter to shame disease, poverty, homes, or bodies. The cleansing rites aim at reintegration, restoration, and careful remediation, not stigma.
